description: Computes a weighted RMS where sample weights decay exponentially from the end of the window (most recent sample has weight 1.0, oldest has weight exp(-decay_rate * window_size)). This emphasizes the onset transient of a squeeze/blink while down-weighting stale samples. The decay rate is tuned per-channel: faster decay for blinks (sharp onset) and slower for squeezes (sustained contraction).
notes: Exponential weighting naturally handles the fact that BCI actions have different temporal profiles. Blinks are fast (~50ms) so high decay_rate focuses on the sharp peak. Squeezes are slower (~200ms) so lower decay_rate integrates over the sustained contraction. onset_bias controls the weight split between first and second half of window.
